comments.text: I’m a 26 year old woman and have been using this for 2 years now. I swear by this product! I don’t have any hair loss disorders, but have always had very thin, fine hair, especially around my hairline. I originally began using this only on my hairline. I didn’t like leaving it in since it did make my hair greasy, so I put it in a few hours before I showered. I have noticed a huge increase in my hair thickness and density. My baby hairs have grown like crazy to even my hairline, I now look like I have full bangs as it’s growing out. I now put it all through my hair before showering and my hair looks so think and full. It even feels heavy when I wash it. It doesn’t make my hair grow faster, but definitely fuller and a little bit darker too (I’m blonde). I use it about once a week. Definitely my holy grail which I recommend to everyone now 💛
comments.author: @cctvbby

comments.text: I have been suffering from some hair loss and have tried a few different things including this. I used it for about 3 months and I actually noticed that it did perpetuate some new hair growth in my problem areas where the hairs continued to grow even after I stopped using it. At the time, I attributed the new growth to scalp oiling, but lately I’ve been thinking that it was actually this product so I’m considering going back to it. I didn’t find it to be greasy as some people have said, but I have very curly hair so my hair can often use some added moisture. I also found that like Dr. Dray said, it lasts a while which is nice. It didn’t have a smell, didn’t make my scalp tingle or burn, and didn’t cause me any irritation (and I tend to have sensitive skin). So, I agree that for the price point it’s worth a try and The Ordinary products are pretty decent. Hope this helps!

comments.text:
I'm using this product at the moment (I have been for about 4-5 months). I started using it when I noticed my hairline was receeding slightly and I wanted to see if this product would help in stopping this.

I apply 4 drops to my scalp and massage a few drops into my hairline before going to bed (to avoid the greasy look, which is one of the only downsides) and repeat this 2-3 times a week. 

I've not only seen a dramatic improvement in my hairline, but also in the reduction of how much of my hair falls out in the shower (it used to be at least a handful each time I washed my hair, this is now only a few strands). This product shocked me in how quickly it began working, my baby hairs in my hairline started popping out of nowhere after about two uses!

I've had multiple compliments on how healthy my hair looks and definitely noticed that my hair feels thicker for the first time in years. 

I should caveat all this by stating I also started taking Wellwoman vitamins around the same time as starting with this product. But the combination of the two has done absolute wonders for my hair, it looks the healthiest it's ever been!

comments.text:
I will add that using procapil and capixl and then stopping will make hair shed like crazy. Redensyl is said to not cause this issue but that didn’t happen with me. I tried this and also Bondi Boost, which also has capixl, procapil, and redensyl in it. I noticed that my hair looked stringy and flat and greasy while using them and when I stopped, my hair shed like crazy. So, I started minoxidil. 9 days into using minoxidil and I was already shedding handfuls right and left and then had to stop using it due to allergic reactions to it. The loss only continued. I’m in a lot of hairloss groups online and there are some undesirable effects of using minoxidil that aren’t commonly discussed, including random dread sheds after using it for a year, frizzy hair, hair breakage, aging facial skin, randomly stopped working, hirsutism, and more. 
I found out that The Ordinary is a cheap product and pushed by influencers who are paid to state that it works. Even their HA was a joke and did zero plumping. I’ve moved over to dermatologist recommended and clinically proven products now and my skin looks amazing from them! 
As for my hair, I’m going to try PRP with pixelate in a few weeks. My skin is so sensitive that I can’t chance another topical that won’t work and that may cause more allergic reactions. 
I greatly appreciate your reviews on products since young, 20-something influencers with already thick hair and no issues don’t provide reliable reviews. I’ve spent enough money trying so many things that don’t work and waste money as have many others who struggle with chronic TE and/or AGA.
